repo_cards.description: Better GitHub statistics images for your profile, with stats from private repos too

readme.text: # [GitHub Stats Visualization](https://github.com/jstrieb/github-stats) Generate visualizations of GitHub user and repository statistics with GitHub Actions. Visualizations can include data for both private repositories, and for repositories you have contributed to, but do not own. Generated images automatically switch between GitHub light theme and GitHub dark theme. ## Background When someone views a profile on GitHub, it is often because they are curious about a user's open source projects and contributions. Unfortunately, that user's stars, forks, and pinned repositories do not necessarily reflect the contributions they make to private repositories. The data likewise does not present a complete picture of the user's total contributions beyond the current year. This project aims to collect a variety of profile and repository statistics using the GitHub API. It then generates images that can be displayed in repository READMEs, or in a user's [Profile README](https://docs.github.com/en/github/setting-up-and-managing-your-github-profile/managing-your-profile-readme). Since the project runs on GitHub Actions, no server is required to regularly regenerate the images with updated statistics. Likewise, since the user runs the analysis code themselves via GitHub Actions, they can use their GitHub access token to collect statistics on private repositories that an external service would be unable to access. ## Disclaimer If the project is used with an access token that has sufficient permissions to read private repositories, it may leak details about those repositories in error messages. For example, the `aiohttp` library—used for asynchronous API requests—may include the requested URL in exceptions, which can leak the name of private repositories. If there is an exception caused by `aiohttp`, this exception will be viewable in the Actions tab of the repository fork, and anyone may be able to see the name of one or more private repositories. Due to some issues with the GitHub statistics API, there are some situations where it returns inaccurate results. Specifically, the repository view count statistics and total lines of code modified are probably somewhat inaccurate. Unexpectedly, these values will become more accurate over time as GitHub caches statistics for your repositories. Additionally, repositories that were last contributed to more than a year ago may not be included in the statistics due to limitations in the results returned by the API. For more information on inaccuracies, see issue [#2](https://github.com/jstrieb/github-stats/issues/2), [#3](https://github.com/jstrieb/github-stats/issues/3), and [#13](https://github.com/jstrieb/github-stats/issues/13). # Installation 1. Create a personal access token (not the default GitHub Actions token) using the instructions [here](https://docs.github.com/en/github/authenticating-to-github/creating-a-personal-access-token). Personal access token must have permissions: `read:user` and `repo`. Copy the access token when it is generated – if you lose it, you will have to regenerate the token. - Some users are reporting that it can take a few minutes for the personal access token to work. For more, see [#30](https://github.com/jstrieb/github-stats/issues/30). 2. Create a copy of this repository by clicking [here](https://github.com/jstrieb/github-stats/generate). Note: this is **not** the same as forking a copy because it copies everything fresh, without the huge commit history. 3. Go to the "Secrets" page of your copy of the repository. If this is the README of your copy, click [this link](../../settings/secrets/actions) to go to the "Secrets" page. Otherwise, go to the "Settings" tab of the newly-created repository and go to the "Secrets" page (bottom left). 4. Create a new secret with the name `ACCESS_TOKEN` and paste the copied personal access token as the value. 5. It is possible to change the type of statistics reported by adding other repository secrets. - To ignore certain repos, add them (in owner/name format e.g., `jstrieb/github-stats`) separated by commas to a new secret—created as before—called `EXCLUDED`. - To ignore certain languages, add them (separated by commas) to a new secret called `EXCLUDED_LANGS`. For example, to exclude HTML and TeX you could set the value to `html,tex`. - To show statistics only for "owned" repositories and not forks with contributions, add an environment variable (under the `env` header in the [main workflow](https://github.com/jstrieb/github-stats/blob/master/.github/workflows/main.yml)) called `EXCLUDE_FORKED_REPOS` with a value of `true`. - These other values are added as secrets by default to prevent leaking information about private repositories. If you're not worried about that, you can change the values directly [in the Actions workflow itself](https://github.com/jstrieb/github-stats/blob/05de1314b870febd44d19ad2f55d5e59d83f5857/.github/workflows/main.yml#L48-L53). 6. Go to the [Actions Page](../../actions?query=workflow%3A"Generate+Stats+Images") and press "Run Workflow" on the right side of the screen to generate images for the first time. - The images will be automatically regenerated every 24 hours, but they can be regenerated manually by running the workflow this way. 7. Take a look at the images that have been created in the [`generated`](generated) folder. 8. To add your statistics to your GitHub Profile README, copy and paste the following lines of code into your markdown content. Change the `username` value to your GitHub username. ```md ![](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/username/github-stats/master/generated/overview.svg#gh-dark-mode-only) ![](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/username/github-stats/master/generated/overview.svg#gh-light-mode-only) ``` ```md ![](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/username/github-stats/master/generated/languages.svg#gh-dark-mode-only) ![](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/username/github-stats/master/generated/languages.svg#gh-light-mode-only) ``` 9. Link back to this repository so that others can generate their own statistics images. 10. Star this repo if you like it! # Support the Project There are a few things you can do to support the project: - Star the repository (and follow me on GitHub for more) - Share and upvote on sites like Twitter, Reddit, and Hacker News - Report any bugs, glitches, or errors that you find These things motivate me to keep sharing what I build, and they provide validation that my work is appreciated! They also help me improve the project. Thanks in advance! If you are insistent on spending money to show your support, I encourage you to instead make a generous donation to one of the following organizations. By advocating for Internet freedoms, organizations like these help me to feel comfortable releasing work publicly on the Web. - [Electronic Frontier Foundation](https://supporters.eff.org/donate/) - [Signal Foundation](https://signal.org/donate/) - [Mozilla](https://donate.mozilla.org/en-US/) - [The Internet Archive](https://archive.org/donate/index.php) # Related Projects - Inspired by a desire to improve upon [anuraghazra/github-readme-stats](https://github.com/anuraghazra/github-readme-stats) - Makes use of [GitHub Octicons](https://primer.style/octicons/) to precisely match the GitHub UI

repo_cards.description: 📘 Get all Warframe items directly from Warframe's API. No more messy wikia scraping.

readme.text:
## Warframe Items [![Supported by Warframe Community Developers](https://warframestat.us/wfcd.png)](https://github.com/WFCD "Supported by Warframe Community Developers") [![npm](https://img.shields.io/npm/v/@wfcd/items.svg)](https://npmjs.org/@wfcd/items) [![warframe update](https://img.shields.io/badge/warframe_update-42.0.4-blue.svg?logo=data%3Aimage%2Fsvg%2Bxml%3Bbase64%2CPHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHdpZHRoPSIyOTgiIGhlaWdodD0iMTczIiB2aWV3Qm94PSIwIDAgMjk4IDE3MyI%%3D%3D)](https://forums.warframe.com/topic/1500079-the-shadowgrapher-hotfix-4204/) [![build](https://github.com/WFCD/warframe-items/actions/workflows/build.yaml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/WFCD/warframe-items/actions/workflows/build.yaml) [![Discord](https://img.shields.io/discord/256087517353213954.svg?logo=discord)](https://discord.gg/jGZxH9f)
Fetches all items available on Warframe's mobile API endpoints while also adding images, drop rates, patch logs and related rivens. The aim of this library is to create a complete collection of data for every item in Warframe based on the game's own data. This repo will be automatically updated on every new release, drop rate change or image change.
### Why use this instead of any other scraper? Because this gives you literally every item in Warframe, many of which can't even be found on the wikia. We also make sure to include every other data aspect that you could possibly need. To give you some idea: - Unique ingame name - (/Lotus/Weapons/Tenno/...) - Especially useful when working with worldState - Drop rates - Patchlogs for each item - Minified source images - Rivens - Whether items are tradable And tons more item specific data. Check out [/data/json](/data/json) to get an idea.
### Installation ``` npm install @wfcd/items ```
### Usage ```js // CommonJS const Items = require('@wfcd/items'); const items = new Items(options, ...oldItems); // ESM import Items from '@wfcd/items'; const items = new Items(options, ...oldItems); // Utilities - Only works in import import { find, colors } from '@wfcd/items/utilities'; const excalPrime = find.findItem('/Lotus/Powersuits/Excalibur/ExcaliburPrime'); // normal items operations now ``` In this example, `items` is an Array with all items. The `...oldItems` param allows you to add your own items before our gathered ones.
### Options | Option | Default | Description | |:------------- |:------------- |:------------- | | category | `['All']` | Array of item categories to retrieve. Parallel to file names in /data/json. Useful if you don't wanna load lots and lots of MB of data into memory. | i18n | `false` | If `false` (default), no internationalization data is loaded. If set to an array of language codes (e.g., `['zh', 'de']`), translations are loaded from `/data/json/i18n.json` and accessible via the main instance's `i18n` field.
**Supported language codes**: `de`, `es`, `fr`, `it`, `ja`, `ko`, `pl`, `pt`, `ru`, `th`, `tr`, `uk`, `zh`. | i18nOnObject | `false` | When `true` and i18n is an array, translation data is attached directly to each item's own `i18n` field instead of being stored centrally. This causes the main instance's `i18n` field to be undefined. | Categories | Description| |:--- | :---- | | All | All categories in one file | | Arcanes | Operator, Zaw, and some Warframe Arcanes | | Archwing | Archwings.... nuff said | | Arch-Gun | Archwing Guns | | Arch-Melee | Archwing Melee weapons | | Enemy | NPCs | | Fish | Fish that you fish| | Gear | Things you equip in your gear wheel | Glyphs | Glyphs from partners, etc. | | Melee | Melee weapons | | Misc | Uncategorized items | | Mods | Modifications for weapons, warframes, archwings, etc. | | Node | Mission nodes | | Pets | Companions, just the beasts | | Primary | Primary slot weapons | | Quests | Quests that you adventure on | | Relics | Things you get prime parts from | | Resources | For building stuff | | Secondary | Secondary slot weapons | | Sentinels | Mechanical companions | | SentinelWeapons | Specialized weapons for sentinels | | Sigils | Chest & back art | | Skins | Henna for your frame | | Warframes | Warframes...|
### Pre-compiled data You can find all automatically compiled data in `/data/json`. Images are stored in `/data/img`. You can find every item's image name stored in `item.imageName`.
### Image links Since there are so many images, and we can't publish them on npm due to the size, we've set up a CDN to get you images at `https://cdn.warframestat.us/img/${item.imageName}` that provides a linkable resource for you. https://runkit.com/tobitenno/615e52157b2af60008dce1f5

## For Developers We're always happy to see contributions to this project, so here's some basic setup information to get you started.
### Dependencies - [Node.js > 10](https://nodejs.org/en/) - [Lua > 5](https://www.lua.org/download.html) - Build Tools (`build-essentials` on linux should be enough, on windows run `npm i -g windows-build-tools`) - libpng-dev12 (linux only)
### File structure All relevant scripts are found in `/build/` with [build.mjs](/build/build.mjs)
The entrypoint for the build script. Here we also save JSON, image and cache data. [scraper.mjs](/build/scraper.mjs)
Fetches all external data and returns it to the parser. [parser.mjs](/build/parser.mjs)
Parses the external data to match our schema and returns it to the build script.
### Usage and Testing You can run the build script with `npm run build`. Once built, you can verify the data with `npm test`. To verify the TypeScript typings are up to date, you can run `npm run typings`. In case you want to skip hash checking in the build process run: `npm run build -- --force` or `npm run build -- -f`. It's also possible to set the environment variable `FORCE='true'`.
## License [MIT](/LICENSE)

readme.text:
[![Test Build](https://img.shields.io/github/actions/workflow/status/UNIkeEN/SJMCL/test.yml?label=test%20build&logo=github&style=for-the-badge)](https://github.com/UNIkeEN/SJMCL/blob/main/.github/workflows/test.yml) ![Downloads](https://img.shields.io/github/downloads/UNIkeEN/SJMCL/total?style=for-the-badge) ![Stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/UNIkeEN/SJMCL?style=for-the-badge) ![Runs](https://img.shields.io/badge/dynamic/json?color=blue&style=for-the-badge&label=runs&query=$.total_count_str&url=https%3A%2F%2Fmc.sjtu.cn%2Fapi-sjmcl%2Fcount) [![Deepwiki](https://img.shields.io/badge/Ask-DeepWiki-20B2AA?logo=&style=for-the-badge)](https://deepwiki.com/UNIkeEN/SJMCL) **English** · [简体中文](docs/README.zh-Hans.md) · [繁體中文](docs/README.zh-Hant.md) ## Features * **Cross Platform**: Supports Windows 10/11, macOS and Linux. * **Efficient Instance Management**: Supports multiple game directories and instances, allowing the management of all instance resources (such as saves, mods, resource packs, shaders, screenshots, etc.) and settings in one place. * **Convenient Resource Download**: Supports downloading game clients, mod loaders, various game resources and modpacks from CurseForge and Modrinth. * **Multi-Account System Support**: Built-in Microsoft login and third-party authentication server support, compatible with the OAuth login process proposed by the Yggdrasil Connect proposal. * **External Service Collaboration**: Works with external webpages, applications, and Agent services through deeplinks and MCP services, delivering a range of convenient features and automation capabilities. * **Open Plugin System**: Supports plugin development to extend the launcher with more interesting and practical features. (Coming Soon) > Note: some features may be limited by region, platform, or bundle type. ### Built with [![Tauri](https://img.shields.io/badge/Tauri-v2-FFC131?style=for-the-badge&logo=tauri&logoColor=white&labelColor=24C8DB)](https://tauri.app/) [![Next JS](https://img.shields.io/badge/next.js-000000?style=for-the-badge&logo=nextdotjs&logoColor=white)](https://nextjs.org/) [![Chakra UI](https://img.shields.io/badge/chakra_ui-v2-38B2AC?style=for-the-badge&logo=chakraui&logoColor=white&labelColor=319795)](https://v2.chakra-ui.com/) ## Getting Started Getting started with SJMCL is simple, just download the latest release from the [Official Website](https://mc.sjtu.cn/sjmcl/en/downloads). You can also find all the releases, including the nightly versions, on [GitHub Releases](https://github.com/UNIkeEN/SJMCL/releases). SJMCL currently supports the following platforms: | Platform | Versions | Architectures | Provided Bundles | |-----------|---------------------|----------------------------|-----------------------------------------| | Windows | 7 and above | `aarch64`, `i686`, `x86_64`| installer `.exe`, portable `.exe` | | macOS | 10.15 and above | `aarch64`, `x86_64` | `.app`, `.dmg` | | Linux | webkit2gtk 4.1 (e.g., Ubuntu 22.04) | `aarch64`, `x86_64` | `.AppImage`, `.deb`, `.rpm`, portable binary | To learn about how to use SJMCL’s features and browse frequently asked questions, please refer to the [User Documentation](https://mc.sjtu.cn/sjmcl/en/docs). ### Windows 7 If you need to run SJMCL on Windows 7, please first [download the Microsoft Edge WebView2 Runtime](https://developer.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-edge/webview2/#download) and install it. We recommend choosing the 'Evergreen Bootstrapper'.

Install from Command Line

Arch Linux

 SJMCL is available on the Arch User Repository (AUR). You can install it using a common [AUR helper](https://wiki.archlinux.org/title/AUR_helpers): ```bash yay -S sjmcl-bin ``` Manual installation without an AUR helper: ```bash git clone https://aur.archlinux.org/sjmcl-bin.git cd sjmcl-bin makepkg -si ```
## Development and Contributing To get started, clone the repository and install the required dependencies: ```bash git clone git@github.com:UNIkeEN/SJMCL.git npm install ``` To run the project in development mode: ```bash npm run tauri dev ``` We warmly invite contributions from everyone. * Before you get started, please take a moment to review our [Contributing Guide](https://github.com/UNIkeEN/SJMCL/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) (includes more details on the development workflow). * API references and some developers’ insights can be found in the [Developer Documentation](https://mc.sjtu.cn/sjmcl/en/dev). * Feel free to share your ideas through [Pull Requests](https://github.com/UNIkeEN/SJMCL/pulls) or [GitHub Issues](https://github.com/UNIkeEN/SJMCL/issues). ### Repo Activity ![Repo Activity](https://repobeats.axiom.co/api/embed/ee2f4be0fbc708179a6b40c83cd8ce80702fe6fe.svg "Repobeats analytics image") ## Copyright Copyright © 2024-2026 SJMCL Team. > NOT AN OFFICIAL MINECRAFT SERVICE. NOT APPROVED BY OR ASSOCIATED WITH MOJANG OR MICROSOFT. The software is distributed under [GNU General Public License v3.0](/LICENSE). By GPLv3 License term 7, we require that when you distribute a modified version of the software, you must obey GPLv3 License as well as the following [additional terms](/LICENSE.EXTRA): 1. Use a different software name than SJMCL or SJMC Launcher; 2. Mark clearly in your repository README file, your distribution website or thread, Support documents, About Page in the software that your program is based on SJMCL and give out the url of the origin repository. 3. When your modifications to this software are limited solely to **adding** (without modifying or deleting) preset authentication servers (`src-tauri/src/account/helpers/authlib_injector/constants.rs`), the restrictions set forth in Clauses 1 above shall not apply. In this case, you may continue to compile and distribute the software under its original name. Besides, per term of use of our website, when distributing a modified version of the software, please send version numbers with prefix (more than two letters, e.g. `XX-0.0.1`) to our statistics server (`src-tauri/src/utils/sys_info.rs`) unless your modifications meets Clauses 3 above. ## Contact Us QQ Group for SJMCL Users: 860851380 You can also send email to [launcher@sjmc.club](mailto:launcher@sjmc.club) if you want to contact us. ## Community Partners We sincerely thank the following organizations for their development and community support throughout the SJMCL project. [ ](https://mc.sjtu.cn/en/)    [](https://www.suesmc.ltd/) [ ](https://www.mualliance.cn/en)      [ ](https://space.bilibili.com/403097853)

evidence.title: README (segment 2)
evidence.excerpt: * **Cross Platform**: Supports Windows 10/11, macOS and Linux. * **Efficient Instance Management**: Supports multiple game directories and instances, allowing the management of all instance resources (such as saves, mods, resource packs, shaders, screenshots, etc.) and settings in one place. * **Convenient Resource Download**: Supports downloading game clients, mod loaders, various game resources and modpacks from CurseForge and Modrinth. * **Multi-Account System Support**: Built-in Microsoft login and third-party authentication server support, compatible with the OAuth login process proposed by the Yggdrasil Connect proposal. * **External Service Collaboration**: Works with external webpages, applications, and Agent services through deeplinks and MCP services, delivering a range of convenient features and automation capabilities. * **Open Plugin System**: Supports plugin development to extend the launcher with more interesting and practical features. (Coming Soon)

repo_cards.description: AutoParams is a versatile test data generator designed for parameterized tests in Java and Kotlin.

items.body_excerpt:
### Added
- Enhanced `InstancePropertyWriter` to detect and handle chaining setters that return the object instance for improved fluent API compatibility

### Changed
- Optimized resolution logging performance by replacing O(n²×d) tree prefix generation with O(n×d) implementation using pre-built tree structure and optimized StringBuilder operations

### Added
- Enhanced `BuilderCustomizer` to support Lombok's `@Singular` annotation for collection fields

items.title: InstancePropertyWriter가 상속받은 set 메서드를 호출하지 않는 문제
Design<T>.set 메서드가 제네릭 형식 속성에 대해 동작하지 않음

evidence.title: README (segment 1)
evidence.excerpt: [![CI](https://github.com/AutoParams/AutoParams/actions/workflows/ci.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/AutoParams/AutoParams/actions/workflows/ci.yml) [![Publish](https://github.com/AutoParams/AutoParams/actions/workflows/publish.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/AutoParams/AutoParams/actions/workflows/publish.yml)

evidence.title: README (segment 2)
evidence.excerpt: **AutoParams** is a JUnit 5 extension for automatic test data generation, inspired by AutoFixture.

evidence.title: README (segment 3)
evidence.excerpt: Manually creating test data is often repetitive and distracts from the core logic of the test. AutoParams eliminates this boilerplate by supplying automatically generated values to your test method parameters, allowing you to write concise and focused tests.

evidence.title: README (segment 4)
evidence.excerpt: Getting started is simple: annotate your `@Test` method with `@AutoParams`, and the parameters will be populated with generated data.

evidence.title: README (segment 5)
evidence.excerpt: ```java @Test @AutoParams void testMethod(int a, int b) { Calculator sut = new Calculator(); int actual = sut.add(a, b); assertEquals(a + b, actual); } ```

evidence.title: README (segment 6)
evidence.excerpt: In the example above, `a` and `b` are automatically generated, making the test cleaner and reducing the need for manual setup or value triangulation.

evidence.title: README (segment 7)
evidence.excerpt: AutoParams also supports more advanced scenarios. When multiple generated values need to share a reference—such as reviews referring to the same product—you can use the `@Freeze` annotation to ensure consistency.

evidence.title: README (segment 8)
evidence.excerpt: private final UUID id; private final String name; private final BigDecimal priceAmount; } ```

evidence.title: InstancePropertyWriter가 상속받은 set 메서드를 호출하지 않는 문제
evidence.excerpt: InstancePropertyWriter가 상속받은 set 메서드를 호출하지 않는 문제 (state:open)

evidence.title: Support for `TypeReference<T>` in the `Design.of` method
evidence.excerpt: Support for `TypeReference<T>` in the `Design.of` method (state:open)

evidence.title: Design<T>.set 메서드가 제네릭 형식 속성에 대해 동작하지 않음
evidence.excerpt: Design<T>.set 메서드가 제네릭 형식 속성에 대해 동작하지 않음 (state:open)

### Added - Enhanced `InstancePropertyWriter` to detect and handle chaining setters that return the object instance for improved fluent API compatibility ### Changed - Optimized resolution logging performance by replacing O(n²×d) tree prefix generation with O(n×d) implementation using pre-built tree structure and optimized StringBuilder operations

### Added - Added `Design` class for fluent object configuration with explicit property values. While `Factory` generates objects with random values, `Design` allows precise control over object properties for predictable test data: ```java // Basic property configuration with fixed and dynamic values Product product = Design.of(Product.class) .set(Product::getName, "Custom Product Name") .supply(Product::getPrice, () -> Math.random() * 100) .instantiate(); ``` The `.set()` method configures properties with constant values, while `.supply()` uses suppliers for dynamic value generation on each instantiation. ```java // Nested object configuration for complex hierarchies Review review = Design.of(Review.class) .set(Review::getRating, 5) .design(Review::getProduct, product -> product .set(Product::getName, "Amazing Widget") .supply(Product::getPrice, () -> random.nextInt(10, 100))) .instantiate(); ``` The `.design()` method enables fluent configuration of nested object properties while maintaining type safety. ```java // Multiple instance creation with identical configurations List<Product> products = design.instantiate(3); ``` Create multiple objects with the same configuration for b…
### Added - Enhanced `BuilderCustomizer` to support Lombok's `@Singular` annotation for collection fields

scout.summary: The repository generates GitHub profile statistics images—including data from private repositories—using GitHub Actions and the GitHub API, with built-in support for light/dark mode. It emphasizes user-controlled execution for privacy but acknowledges risks of leaking private repo names via error messages and known inaccuracies in GitHub's underlying statistics API.
highlights: Generates customizable statistics images for GitHub profiles using GitHub Actions, supporting both public and private repositories.
Images automatically adapt to GitHub's light and dark themes using CSS media query-compatible image tags.
No external server required; runs entirely via GitHub Actions using the user's own access token.
Acknowledges limitations in GitHub API data, such as inaccurate view counts, missing old repositories, and improving accuracy over time due to caching.
risks: Potential leakage of private repository names through exception messages (e.g., from aiohttp) visible in GitHub Actions logs.
Reliance on potentially inaccurate or incomplete data from GitHub’s statistics API, especially for older or less active repositories.

inventor.summary: The repository invents a self-hosted, privacy-aware method to generate personalized GitHub statistics images—including data from private repositories—by leveraging GitHub Actions and the user's own access token, while addressing theme adaptability and acknowledging inherent API limitations.
highlights: Invents a workflow that combines GitHub Actions with user-provided tokens to securely gather stats from both public and private repositories without external servers.
Introduces theme-responsive SVG images that dynamically switch between light and dark modes using GitHub-specific CSS media query anchors in image URLs.
Empowers users to showcase comprehensive contribution metrics beyond GitHub’s native profile view, including historical and private repo activity.
risks: Private repository names may be exposed in GitHub Actions logs if aiohttp exceptions occur during API calls, due to URL leakage in error messages.
Statistical inaccuracies are unavoidable due to known flaws in GitHub’s API, such as unreliable view counts and exclusion of repositories inactive for over a year.

engineer.summary: The repository implements a GitHub Actions-based pipeline in Python to generate theme-aware SVG statistics images using the GitHub API, supporting private repositories through user-provided tokens while acknowledging data inaccuracies and potential private repo name leakage via exception logs.
highlights: Core logic implemented in Python scripts (`github_stats.py`, `generate_images.py`) with dependencies listed in `requirements.txt` [ID:E-RF-001].
Generates SVG images that dynamically switch between light/dark themes using GitHub-specific URL fragments (#gh-light-mode-only / #gh-dark-mode-only) [ID:E-RD-001][ID:E-RD-003].
Runs entirely on GitHub Actions without external servers, enabling access to private repository data via the user's own token [ID:E-RD-006].
Explicitly documents known limitations of GitHub’s API, including inaccurate view counts, missing old repositories, and gradual data improvement via caching [ID:E-RD-008].
risks: Private repository names may be exposed in GitHub Actions logs if `aiohttp` exceptions occur, as error messages can include full request URLs containing private repo identifiers [ID:E-RD-007].
Statistical outputs are inherently constrained by GitHub API inaccuracies, particularly for repositories inactive over a year or metrics like view counts and lines of code [ID:E-RD-008].

skeptic.summary: While the repository promises enhanced GitHub statistics including private repo data via self-hosted Actions, it relies on an inherently flawed and incomplete GitHub API, exposes sensitive information through poor error handling, and lacks architectural separation between code and output—raising concerns about maintainability, accuracy, and security.
highlights: Claims to provide comprehensive stats but openly admits GitHub’s API returns inaccurate view counts, excludes old repositories, and only improves gradually via caching [ID:E-RD-008].
Uses fragile error-prone mechanisms that can leak private repository names in public-facing GitHub Actions logs due to unguarded aiohttp exceptions [ID:E-RD-007].

Mixes application code and generated image artifacts in the same repository (e.g., `generated/` directory), violating separation of concerns and complicating reuse or packaging [ID:E-RF-001][ID:E-IS-001].
risks: Users may falsely assume their private repository metadata is fully protected, when in fact exception logs can expose repo names to anyone with access to the Actions tab—even in public forks.
Statistical outputs are presented as meaningful metrics despite known inaccuracies, potentially misleading users into making decisions based on unreliable data.
The monolithic structure (code + generated assets) and lack of formal packaging hinder adoption as a reusable action or library, as noted in open issue [ID:E-IS-001].

scout.summary: The WFCD/warframe-items repository is a well-maintained JavaScript library that provides comprehensive, up-to-date Warframe item data sourced directly from official APIs, including drop rates, patch logs, rivens, and in-game identifiers. It is actively updated with automated releases and supports both CommonJS and ESM usage.
highlights: Provides complete Warframe item data directly from official mobile API endpoints, avoiding reliance on wikia scraping [ID:E-RD-003].
Automatically updated on every game release or data change, ensuring freshness [ID:E-RD-004].
Includes unique in-game identifiers (e.g., /Lotus/...), drop rates, patch logs, tradability status, and riven data [ID:E-RD-006].
Frequent automated releases (e.g., v1.1272.170 on 2026-03-31) primarily for new item additions [ID:E-RL-001, E-RL-002, E-RL-003].
Supports modern JavaScript tooling with clear entry points (index.js, index.mjs) and dev dependencies like ESLint, Husky, and Mocha [ID:E-RF-001].
risks: Open issue indicates potential compatibility problems on Windows NTFS filesystems during git operations [ID:E-IS-001].

inventor.summary: The repository invents a structured, automated pipeline to transform raw Warframe game data into a developer-friendly, comprehensive item database with enriched metadata not easily obtainable elsewhere.
highlights: Invents a novel aggregation method by combining official mobile API data with supplemental features like drop rates, patch logs, rivens, and tradability flags [ID:E-RD-003, E-RD-006].
Introduces unique in-game path identifiers (e.g., /Lotus/Weapons/...) as primary keys, enabling precise integration with Warframe's internal systems like worldState [ID:E-RD-006].
Automates data updates via CI/CD-triggered releases synchronized with Warframe game patches, ensuring real-time accuracy without manual curation [ID:E-RD-004, E-RL-001, E-RL-002, E-RL-003].
Provides both CommonJS and ESM entry points (index.js, index.mjs), demonstrating intentional design for broad compatibility across modern and legacy JavaScript environments [ID:E-RF-001, E-RD-008].
risks: Reliance on undocumented or unstable mobile API endpoints could break the pipeline if Digital Extremes changes their internal data structure [ID:E-RD-003].
Filesystem-specific issues (e.g., Windows NTFS) may hinder contributor onboarding or local development workflows [ID:E-IS-001].

engineer.summary: The repository is a robust, automated JavaScript library that ingests and enriches Warframe item data from official APIs, offering comprehensive, structured, and up-to-date information with strong tooling support and frequent releases.
highlights: Data is sourced directly from Warframe's mobile API endpoints and enriched with drop rates, patch logs, rivens, tradability, and unique in-game paths like /Lotus/... for precise integration [ID:E-RD-003, E-RD-006].

Fully automated release pipeline ensures immediate updates after game patches, as evidenced by consecutive daily releases (v1.1272.168 to v1.1272.170) adding new items [ID:E-RL-001, E-RL-002, E-RL-003].
Modern JavaScript engineering practices are evident: dual CommonJS/ESM entry points (index.js, index.mjs), linting (ESLint), commit hooks (Husky), and testing (Mocha) [ID:E-RF-001, E-RD-008].
Explicit design goal to avoid wikia scraping by providing complete, canonical item data not otherwise accessible [ID:E-RD-004, E-RD-005].
risks: An open issue indicates git operations may fail on Windows NTFS due to filename restrictions, potentially blocking contributors or CI environments using that filesystem [ID:E-IS-001].
Heavy reliance on internal Warframe API endpoints introduces fragility; any undocumented change by the game developer could break data ingestion without warning [ID:E-RD-003].

skeptic.summary: While the repository claims comprehensive and automated Warframe item data sourcing, its reliability hinges on unstable assumptions about API stability, filesystem compatibility, and actual data completeness—claims that lack sufficient verification or fallback mechanisms.
highlights: Claims of 'complete' item data sourced directly from Warframe's mobile API [ID:E-RD-003, E-RD-004] are unverifiable without documentation of endpoint contracts or schema guarantees.
Automated daily releases (e.g., v1.1272.168–170) only confirm new item additions, not validation of data integrity or correctness [ID:E-RL-001, E-RL-002, E-RL-003].
The assertion that this library provides data 'not found on wikia' [ID:E-RD-005] is unsubstantiated and lacks comparative evidence.
risks: Critical dependency on undocumented mobile API endpoints creates a single point of failure; any change by Digital Extremes could silently corrupt or halt data ingestion [ID:E-RD-003].
Open issue about Windows NTFS git failures [ID:E-IS-001] suggests poor cross-platform robustness, undermining claims of broad usability.
No evidence of schema validation, testing coverage for data accuracy, or error handling in ingestion pipelines—only tooling for code linting and commits [ID:E-RF-001].

scout.summary: SJMCL is a cross-platform, open-source Minecraft launcher built with Tauri and Next.js, offering advanced instance management, modpack installation from CurseForge/Modrinth, multi-account support, and plugin extensibility. It targets Windows, macOS, and Linux with multiple distribution formats, but remains in active beta development with known stability and UX issues.
highlights: Built with modern stack: Tauri v2 + Next.js + Chakra UI [ID:E-RD-003]
Supports cross-platform deployment (Windows 7+, macOS 10.15+, Linux) with diverse installers [ID:E-RD-006]
Features rich Minecraft ecosystem integration: mod loaders, CurseForge/Modrinth downloads, Optifine, and resource management [ID:E-RD-002]
Offers external service collaboration via deeplinks and MCP, plus an open plugin system [ID:E-RD-002]
Provides official documentation and download channels beyond GitHub [ID:E-RD-004, E-RD-007]
risks: Still in pre-release beta (v1.0.0-beta.x); nightly builds explicitly discouraged for regular users [ID:E-RL-001, E-RL-002, E-RL-003]
Active open bugs affecting core functionality: mod loader installation failures and log copying issues [ID:E-IS-001, E-IS-003]
Historical instability in mod installation workflows (e.g., Optifine state machine bug) [ID:E-IS-002]
Windows 7 requires manual WebView2 Runtime installation, adding setup friction [ID:E-RD-008]

inventor.summary: SJMCL is an inventive cross-platform Minecraft launcher that integrates modern web and desktop technologies to deliver a unified, extensible experience for managing game instances, mods, and accounts. It leverages Tauri v2 for native performance with a Next.js frontend, enabling rich features like external service collaboration, OAuth-compatible authentication, and plugin-based extensibility—while supporting diverse deployment formats across Windows, macOS, and Linux.
highlights: Innovative fusion of Tauri v2 (desktop) and Next.js (web UI) creates a performant, responsive launcher architecture [ID:E-RD-003]
Unified instance management system centralizes saves, mods, shaders, and settings across multiple Minecraft installations [ID:E-RD-002]
Pioneers integration with external ecosystems via deeplinks and MCP services, enabling automation and third-party app interoperability [ID:E-RD-002]
Supports both CurseForge and Modrinth natively for modpack and resource downloads—a rare dual-platform capability [ID:E-RD-002]
Implements Yggdrasil Connect–compatible OAuth flow, offering flexible multi-account support beyond standard Microsoft auth [ID:E-RD-002]
risks: Core innovation in mod installation workflows remains unstable, as evidenced by recurring failures with mod loaders and Optifine [ID:E-IS-001, E-IS-002]
Beta-stage maturity limits real-world reliability; nightly and beta releases carry explicit warnings against production use [ID:E-RL-001, E-RL-002, E-RL-003]
Advanced features like plugin system and external service links lack visible documentation or community adoption signals [ID:E-RD-002]
Windows 7 compatibility requires manual runtime dependency (WebView2), undermining seamless cross-platform promise [ID:E-RD-008]

engineer.summary: SJMCL is a Tauri-based Minecraft launcher with a Next.js frontend, offering cross-platform support and advanced mod/resource management. While it demonstrates solid architectural choices and feature breadth, it remains in beta with unresolved stability issues in core workflows like mod loader installation and log handling.
highlights: Uses Tauri v2 + Next.js + Chakra UI for a modern, cross-platform desktop app architecture [ID:E-RD-003]
Supports diverse deployment formats per platform: .exe/.AppImage/.dmg etc., with clear compatibility specs [ID:E-RD-006]
Integrates CurseForge and Modrinth for modpack/resource downloads, reducing manual user effort [ID:E-RD-002]
Includes structured project setup with dev tooling (.eslintrc.json, .prettierrc, husky) and internationalization config [ID:E-RF-001]
Provides official docs and external download channels, indicating investment in user onboarding [ID:E-RD-004, E-RD-007]
risks: Critical mod loader installation failures remain unresolved (e.g., FarmingTales modpack) [ID:E-IS-001]
Log viewer has data integrity issues during copy operations, impacting debugging usability [ID:E-IS-003]
Beta and nightly releases explicitly warn against production use; no stable v1.0 yet [ID:E-RL-001, E-RL-002, E-RL-003]
Windows 7 requires out-of-band WebView2 Runtime install, complicating deployment [ID:E-RD-008]
Past state machine bugs in Optifine installer suggest fragile installation logic [ID:E-IS-002]

skeptic.summary: SJMCL presents an ambitious feature set but lacks evidence of stability, maturity, or reliable core functionality—despite polished marketing in its README. Persistent unresolved bugs, pre-release status, and dependency caveats undermine its readiness for general use.
highlights: README heavily emphasizes features and badges (downloads, stars, CI) while downplaying immaturity [ID:E-RD-001, E-RD-002]
Claims cross-platform support but imposes manual WebView2 installation on Windows 7 users, contradicting 'just works' messaging [ID:E-RD-008]
Advertises modpack installation from major platforms yet fails on common cases like FarmingTales [ID:E-IS-001, E-RD-002]
risks: No stable release exists; all available versions are explicitly labeled as beta or nightly with warnings against regular use [ID:E-RL-001, E-RL-002, E-RL-003]
Critical user-facing bugs remain open for months, including mod loader failures and data loss during log copying [ID:E-IS-001, E-IS-003]
Past issues like Optifine state machine errors suggest systemic flaws in installation logic, not isolated edge cases [ID:E-IS-002]
Overreliance on external services (CurseForge, Modrinth, OAuth servers) increases failure surface without clear fallbacks or error resilience

synth.wire_raw:
{
  "app": {
    "name": "SJMCL",
    "one_sentence": "A cross-platform Minecraft launcher with advanced instance management, modpack installation from CurseForge/Modrinth, multi-account support, and plugin extensibility.",

scout.summary: AutoParams is a JUnit 5 extension for automatic test data generation in Java and Kotlin, inspired by AutoFixture. It reduces boilerplate by injecting generated values into test parameters and supports advanced features like frozen references and fluent object design via the `Design` class.
highlights: Provides seamless auto-generation of test parameters using `@AutoParams`, improving test conciseness and focus.
Introduces `Design` class (v11.3.0) for precise, fluent configuration of test objects with fixed or dynamic properties.
Actively maintained with recent performance optimizations (e.g., O(n×d) logging) and support for Lombok’s `@Singular`.
Supports complex scenarios like shared object references via `@Freeze` and nested object configuration.
risks: Open issues indicate unresolved bugs in core functionality: `InstancePropertyWriter` fails to call inherited setters [ID:E-IS-001].
Known limitations in generic type handling: `Design<T>.set` does not work correctly with generic properties [ID:E-IS-003].
Missing support for `TypeReference<T>` in `Design.of`, limiting flexibility with complex generic types [ID:E-IS-002].

inventor.summary: AutoParams introduces innovative mechanisms for automated test data generation in JUnit 5, notably through the `@AutoParams` annotation and the fluent `Design` API for precise object construction, enabling expressive and maintainable parameterized tests.
highlights: Pioneers a fluent `Design` API (v11.3.0) that allows fine-grained control over test object instantiation with `.set()` for fixed values and `.supply()` for dynamic generation, supporting nested configurations [ID:E-RL-002].
Eliminates test boilerplate by auto-injecting generated parameters into test methods via `@AutoParams`, enhancing test clarity and reducing manual setup [ID:E-RD-003][ID:E-RD-004][ID:E-RD-005].
Enables consistent object references across test parameters using `@Freeze`, facilitating realistic scenarios like multiple reviews tied to the same product [ID:E-RD-007].
Optimizes internal performance with an O(n×d) resolution logging algorithm and enhances compatibility with fluent APIs by improving `InstancePropertyWriter` setter detection [ID:E-RL-001].
risks: Core object population logic is flawed: `InstancePropertyWriter` fails to invoke inherited setter methods, potentially breaking data injection for common inheritance-based designs [ID:E-IS-001].
The `Design<T>.set` method does not correctly handle generic-typed properties, limiting its utility in modern Java codebases that rely on generics [ID:E-IS-003].
Lack of `TypeReference<T>` support in `Design.of` restricts usage with complex generic types (e.g., nested parameterized types), reducing flexibility [ID:E-IS-002].

engineer.summary: AutoParams is a JUnit 5 extension that automates test data generation in Java/Kotlin, reducing boilerplate via @AutoParams and enabling precise object configuration through the fluent Design API. Recent releases include performance improvements and enhanced setter handling, but open issues reveal critical gaps in generic type support and inheritance-aware property writing.
highlights: Automatically injects generated test parameters using @AutoParams, streamlining test methods and eliminating manual data setup [ID:E-RD-004][ID:E-RD-005].
Provides the Design class (v11.3.0) for fluent, fine-grained object construction with .set() for constants and .supply() for dynamic values, including nested object support [ID:E-RL-002].
Optimized internal resolution logging from O(n²×d) to O(n×d) and improved InstancePropertyWriter to handle fluent chaining setters [ID:E-RL-001].
Supports shared object references across parameters via @Freeze, enabling realistic test scenarios like multiple reviews for the same product [ID:E-RD-007].
risks: InstancePropertyWriter fails to invoke inherited setter methods, breaking data population in class hierarchies [ID:E-IS-001].
Design<T>.set does not work correctly with generic-typed properties, limiting usability in generic-heavy codebases [ID:E-IS-003].
Missing TypeReference<T> support in Design.of restricts creation of objects with complex generic signatures (e.g., List<Map<String, T>>) [ID:E-IS-002].

skeptic.summary: While AutoParams promises streamlined test data generation and fluent object configuration, critical unresolved bugs in core functionality—particularly around inheritance and generics—undermine its reliability for real-world Java applications.
highlights: Claims of seamless test automation via @AutoParams are contradicted by open issues showing fundamental flaws in property population logic [ID:E-IS-001].
The touted Design API’s precision is limited by its inability to handle generic properties correctly, reducing its effectiveness in modern Java code [ID:E-IS-003].
Despite recent performance optimizations [ID:E-RL-001], the library lacks support for TypeReference<T>, a basic requirement for working with complex generic types [ID:E-IS-002].
risks: InstancePropertyWriter’s failure to call inherited setters [ID:E-IS-001] suggests inadequate handling of common OOP patterns, risking silent test failures or incomplete object initialization.
Design<T>.set not working with generic-typed properties [ID:E-IS-003] indicates shallow type introspection, which could lead to incorrect test data or runtime errors in generic-heavy systems.
Absence of TypeReference<T> support in Design.of [ID:E-IS-002] severely limits usability with parameterized types like List<Map<String, T>>, a common pattern in enterprise Java.
